title: Kubernetes Pod Status Summary
description:
Parse `kubectl get pods` output. Return:

```
total N
running N
not_running N
high_restarts name:count,...
```

`high_restarts` lists pods with RESTARTS > 5 as `name:count` sorted by count descending (empty string after the key if none). Skip the header line.
constraints: Input is a plain string passed to solve(input string) string
Return a string matching the expected output exactly
Return empty string where the problem specifies no results

examples.input:
NAME                    READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE
web-7d9f8b-xkr2p         1/1     Running          2   1d
api-5c8d4f-lmn3q         0/1     Pending          0   2h
db-6e7f9g-pqr4s          1/1     Running         12   3d
worker-8h9i0j-tuv5w      0/1     Error            7   5h
examples.explanation: See test case: 4 pods, 2 running, 2 high-restart

test_cases.input:
NAME                    READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE
web-7d9f8b-xkr2p         1/1     Running          2   1d
api-5c8d4f-lmn3q         0/1     Pending          0   2h
db-6e7f9g-pqr4s          1/1     Running         12   3d
worker-8h9i0j-tuv5w      0/1     Error            7   5h

NAME     READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE
pod-1      1/1     Running          0   1h
pod-2      1/1     Running          1   2h
